channel.title: Snapshot on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Snapshot on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Git Archive Creation Error
item.description:
What This Error Means

A Git archive error occurs when git archive fails to create a snapshot of the repository. This command exports the contents of a tree without the .git directory, but can fail due to missing refs, invalid format options, or file system issues.

Common Causes

  • The specified branch, tag, or commit does not exist
  • Invalid output format specified
  • Output file path is not writable
  • Disk space is insufficient
  • The archive format does not match the output file extension

How to Fix

Verify the Ref Exists

git log --oneline -1 main
git tag -l

Create a Tar Archive

git archive --format=tar HEAD -o snapshot.tar

Create a Zip Archive

git archive --format=zip HEAD -o snapshot.zip

Archive a Specific Branch or Tag

git archive --format=zip v1.0.0 -o release-v1.0.0.zip

Archive a Subdirectory

git archive --format=zip HEAD src/ -o src-only.zip

Check Disk Space

df -h

Examples

# Example 1: Archive current branch
git archive HEAD -o project.tar.gz

# Example 2: Archive a tag
git archive v2.1.0 --format=zip -o v2.1.0.zip

# Example 3: Archive with prefix
git archive --prefix=myproject-v1/ HEAD -o project.tar
tar -tf project.tar
# myproject-v1/src/main.js
# myproject-v1/README.md

# Example 4: List available formats
git archive --list
# tar
# zip

Related Errors

  • Git Fetch Error — fetch failed
  • Git Tag Error — tag creation error
  • Git Branch Error — branch operation error

item.title: [Solution] macOS Time Machine Backup Error
item.description:
macOS Time Machine Backup Error Fix

Time Machine errors include backups failing to start, “Backup disk not available,” incomplete backups, or “Unable to backup” messages. Time Machine is macOS’s built-in backup solution.

What This Error Means

Time Machine creates hourly snapshots and full backups to a designated disk. Failures occur when the backup disk is disconnected, the disk image is corrupt, or the backup database is damaged.

Common Causes

  • Backup disk disconnected or powered off during backup
  • Backup disk full or running low on space
  • Corrupt Time Machine backup database
  • Network backup destination unreachable (NAS/network drive)
  • File system corruption on backup disk
  • macOS update changing Time Machine behavior

How to Fix

1. Check backup disk status

# List connected disks
diskutil list

# Check if backup disk is mounted
ls /Volumes/

# Verify Time Machine backup disk
tmutil listbackups

2. Delete corrupt local snapshots

# List local snapshots
tmutil listlocalsnapshots /

# Delete specific snapshot
tmutil deletelocalsnapshots <date>

# Delete all local snapshots
for snapshot in $(tmutil listlocalsnapshots / | awk -F'.' '{print $NF}'); do
 tmutil deletelocalsnapshots $snapshot
done

3. Verify and repair the backup disk

# Verify the backup disk
diskutil verifyVolume /Volumes/BackupDisk

# Repair if needed
diskutil repairVolume /Volumes/BackupDisk

4. Reset Time Machine

# Stop Time Machine
tmutil disable

# Delete the backup database (CAUTION: this erases all backups)
sudo rm -rf /Volumes/BackupDisk/Backups.backupdb

# Re-enable and start fresh
tmutil enable
tmutil startbackup

Related Errors

  • Disk Utility Error — disk repair and verification errors
  • iCloud Error — cloud backup alternative issues
  • Finder Error — file system errors affecting backups

item.title: [Solution] ORA-01555: Snapshot Too Old
item.description:
ORA-01555: Snapshot Too Old

ORA-01555 occurs when a long-running query needs to read data that has been overwritten in the undo (rollback) tablespace. The undo data needed for consistent read is no longer available.

Common Causes

  • The undo tablespace is too small for the workload
  • A long-running query references data modified by concurrent transactions
  • The UNDO_RETENTION setting is too low
  • The undo tablespace has autoextend disabled

How to Fix

Increase Undo Retention

ALTER SYSTEM SET undo_retention = 3600; -- 1 hour

Resize the Undo Tablespace

ALTER DATABASE DATAFILE '/u01/oradata/undotbs01.dbf' RESIZE 20G;
-- or add a new datafile
ALTER TABLESPACE undotbs1 ADD DATAFILE '/u01/oradata/undotbs02.dbf'
 SIZE 10G AUTOEXTEND ON;

Enable Guaranteed Undo Retention

ALTER TABLESPACE undotbs1 RETENTION GUARANTEE;

Optimize Long-Running Queries

-- Break long queries into smaller batches
-- Instead of:
SELECT * FROM large_table;

-- Use:
SELECT * FROM large_table WHERE id BETWEEN 1 AND 100000;
SELECT * FROM large_table WHERE id BETWEEN 100001 AND 200000;

Check Current Undo Usage

SELECT tablespace_name, SUM(bytes) / 1024 / 1024 AS mb
FROM dba_undo_extents
GROUP BY tablespace_name;

Examples

-- Long-running report
SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(4) */ * FROM sales WHERE quarter = 'Q1';
-- ORA-01555: snapshot too old: rollback segment number 5 with name "_SYSSMU5$"
-- Fix: increase undo_retention or optimize the query

-- Concurrent DML during report
-- Session 1: long query
-- Session 2: DELETE FROM sales WHERE ...
-- Fix: use UNDO_RETENTION GUARANTEE

Related Errors

  • ORA-01652 — temp segment extension failure
  • ORA-08177 — serialization access failure

item.title: Maven Release SNAPSHOT Version Error
item.description:
Maven Release — SNAPSHOT Version Error

This error occurs when the Maven release plugin encounters issues with SNAPSHOT versions. The release process fails because of SNAPSHOT dependencies, uncommitted changes, or incorrect version configuration.

Common Causes

  • Project contains SNAPSHOT dependencies
  • Working directory has uncommitted changes
  • Release version already exists in repository
  • SCM connection not configured
  • Version number format is invalid

How to Fix

Prepare the Release

mvn release:prepare -DdevelopmentVersion=1.1-SNAPSHOT -DreleaseVersion=1.0

Check for SNAPSHOT Dependencies

mvn dependency:tree | grep SNAPSHOT

Remove SNAPSHOT Versions

<dependency>
 <groupId>com.example</groupId>
 <artifactId>library</artifactId>
 <version>1.0.0</version> <!-- use release version -->
</dependency>

Clean Up Failed Release

mvn release:clean

Configure Release Plugin

<plugin>
 <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
 <artifactId>maven-release-plugin</artifactId>
 <version>3.0.1</version>
 <configuration>
 <tagNameFormat>v@{project.version}</tagNameFormat>
 <autoVersionSubmodules>true</autoVersionSubmodules>
 </configuration>
</plugin>

Commit Changes Before Release

git status
git add -A
git commit -m "Prepare for release"
mvn release:prepare release:perform

Examples

[ERROR] The repository contains uncommitted changes.
[ERROR] Cannot prepare a release version when the POM contains SNAPSHOT dependencies.
[ERROR] Could not find the SCM URL in the POM.

Related Errors

  • Maven Dependency Error — dependency resolution failure
  • Maven Multi Module Error — reactor build issues
  • Maven Build Error — general build failure

item.title: Oracle - ORA-01555: snapshot too old
item.description:
ORA-01555: snapshot too old (rollback segment too small) occurs when Oracle cannot maintain a read-consistent view of data because the undo information needed by a long-running query has been overwritten by newer transactions.

Common Causes

  • Long-running query spanning many DML operations
  • UNDO tablespace too small to retain enough history
  • FETCH across COMMIT in cursor operations
  • Batch processing without periodic commits
  • UNDO_RETENTION too short for the workload

How to Fix

  1. Check UNDO tablespace usage:
SELECT tablespace_name, round(sum_bytes/1024/1024/1024, 2) as size_gb
FROM (
 SELECT tablespace_name, sum(bytes) as sum_bytes
 FROM dba_data_files
 WHERE tablespace_name = (SELECT value FROM v$parameter WHERE name = 'undo_tablespace')
 GROUP BY tablespace_name
);
  1. Increase UNDO tablespace size:
ALTER DATABASE DATAFILE '/u01/oradata/mydb/undotbs01.dbf'
AUTOEXTEND ON NEXT 1G MAXSIZE 30G;
  1. Increase UNDO retention:
ALTER SYSTEM SET UNDO_RETENTION = 3600; -- 1 hour
  1. Use FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for concurrent processing:
DECLARE
 CURSOR c_orders IS
 SELECT order_id FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'
 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;
BEGIN
 FOR rec IN c_orders LOOP
 -- process without blocking
 UPDATE orders SET status = 'processing' WHERE order_id = rec.order_id;
 COMMIT;
 END LOOP;
END;
  1. Optimize long-running queries:
-- Add indexes to reduce query time
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);

-- Use parallel query for large scans
SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(4) */ * FROM large_table;
  1. Increase PGA for sort operations:
ALTER SYSTEM SET pga_aggregate_target = 4G;

Examples

-- Error: ORA-01555: snapshot too old: rollback segment number 15 with name "_SYSSMU15$" too small
DECLARE
 CURSOR c IS SELECT * FROM large_table;
BEGIN
 FOR rec IN c LOOP
 DBMS_LOCK.SLEEP(1); -- simulate slow processing
 -- Other sessions modifying large_table
 END LOOP;
END;

-- Fix: commit periodically or increase UNDO
ALTER SYSTEM SET UNDO_RETENTION = 7200;

Related Errors

  • Lock error
  • Tablespace error

item.title: Redis - RDB save failed
item.description:
An RDB save failed error occurs when Redis cannot complete a background save to create an RDB snapshot. This can result in data loss if the server restarts before a successful save completes.

Common Causes

  • Disk space exhaustion on the data volume
  • Disk I/O latency or failure
  • fork() failure due to insufficient memory for background save
  • RDB file path not writable
  • Large dataset causing save to take too long

How to Fix

  1. Check disk space:
df -h /var/lib/redis
du -sh /var/lib/redis/dump.rdb
  1. Free up disk space:
# Remove old RDB files
rm -f /var/lib/redis/dump.rdb.bak

# Check Redis log for save errors
tail -100 /var/log/redis/redis.log
  1. Check RDB configuration:
# redis.conf
save 900 1 # save after 900 seconds if at least 1 key changed
save 300 10 # save after 300 seconds if at least 10 keys changed
save 60 10000 # save after 60 seconds if at least 10000 keys changed

dir /var/lib/redis
dbfilename dump.rdb
  1. Trigger manual save for testing:
redis-cli BGSAVE
# Check status
redis-cli LASTSAVE
redis-cli INFO persistence
  1. Ensure sufficient memory for fork:
# Redis needs ~2x memory during fork for RDB
# Check available memory
free -h

# If low memory, reduce dataset or increase RAM
  1. Switch to AOF for more reliable persistence:
# redis.conf
appendonly yes
appendfsync everysec

Examples

# Error: Failed opening the RDB dump file for saving: No space left on device
$ redis-cli BGSAVE
Background save failed: No space left on device

# Fix: free disk space and retry
$ rm -f /var/log/redis/*.gz
$ redis-cli BGSAVE
OK

Related Errors

  • Memory error
  • Cluster error

item.title: Redis Persistence Error
item.description:
Redis Persistence Error

A Redis persistence error occurs when Redis fails to save data to disk through RDB snapshots or AOF (Append-Only File) logging. This can result in data loss after restart.

Common Causes

  • Disk space exhaustion
  • RDB fork fails due to memory issues
  • AOF file corruption
  • Background save process killed

How to Fix

Check Disk Space

df -h /var/lib/redis

Check Persistence Configuration

# /etc/redis/redis.conf
# RDB snapshot
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000

# AOF
appendonly yes
appendfsync everysec

Monitor Background Saves

redis-cli LASTSAVE
redis-cli INFO persistence

Fix AOF Corruption

redis-check-aof --fix appendonly.aof

Fix RDB Corruption

redis-check-rdb dump.rdb

Disable Persistence Temporarily

# /etc/redis/redis.conf
save ""
appendonly no

Restart Redis After Fix

sudo systemctl restart redis-server

Examples

redis-cli BGSAVE
Background saving started
# Later in logs:
# MISCONF Redis is configured to save RDB snapshots, but is unable to.
# Cannot open the append-only file: Permission denied

Related Errors

  • Memory Error — out of memory
  • Cluster Error — cluster issues
